data:
Medals from Clan War Leagues are split into base medals, which every roster player can earn, and bonus medals, which leaders can award to top performers at the end of the CWL season.
Base medals depend on the league tier, the clan’s final placement, and the stars a player earns. Each player receives a percentage of the clan’s maximum medal payout based on their total stars across the 7 wars.
data.bonus-medals#Bonus medals: League Medals per bonus Number of bonuses Unranked 0 0 Bronze League III 42 1 Bronze League II 45 1 Bronze League I 48 1 Silver League III 51 1 Silver League II 54 1 Silver League I 57 1 Gold League III 60 2 Gold League II 63 2 Gold League I 66 2 Crystal League III 69 2 Crystal League II 72 2 Crystal League I 75 2 Master League III 78 3 Master League II 81 3 Master League I 84 3 Champion League III 87 4 Champion League II 90 4 Champion League I 93 4 Titan League III 96 5 Titan League II 99 5 Titan League I 102 5 Legend League 105 6 Too difficult? Let War Report do the math for you.
data.medals-for-clan-position#Medals for clan: League 1st 2nd 3rd 4th 5th 6th 7th 8th Unranked 0 0 0 0 0 0 0 0 Bronze League III 46 44 42 40 38 36 34 32 Bronze League II 58 56 54 52 50 48 46 44 Bronze League I 70 68 66 64 62 60 58 56 Silver League III 88 85 82 79 76 73 70 67 Silver League II 106 103 100 97 94 91 88 85 Silver League I 124 121 118 115 112 109 106 103 Gold League III 148 144 140 136 132 128 124 120 Gold League II 172 168 164 160 156 152 148 144 Gold League I 196 192 188 184 180 176 172 168 Crystal League III 220 216 212 208 204 200 196 192 Crystal League II 244 240 236 232 228 224 220 216 Crystal League I 274 269 264 259 254 249 244 239 Master League III 304 299 294 289 284 279 274 269 Master League II 334 329 324 319 314 309 304 299 Master League I 364 359 354 349 344 339 334 329 Champion League III 388 384 380 376 372 368 364 360 Champion League II 412 408 404 400 396 392 388 384 Champion League I 436 432 428 424 420 416 412 408 Titan League III 454 451 448 445 442 439 436 433 Titan League II 472 469 466 463 460 457 454 451 Titan League I 490 487 484 481 478 475 472 469 Legend League 508 505 502 499 496 493 490 487 Bonus medals are tied directly to league tier and the number of wars your clan wins. Each tier has a guaranteed number of bonus slots with a fixed medal value per slot, and every war win adds one additional bonus slot on top of that guaranteed amount.

data:
The following table provides an overview of the most important recurring events in Clash of Clans and their typical dates and times in UTC.
Event Frequency Date Clan War Leagues Monthly Sign-up starts on the 1st of the month (08:00 UTC) Clan Games Monthly 22nd–28th (08:00 UTC) Gold Pass Season Monthly Season starts on the 1st of the month (08:00 UTC) Legend League Monthly Season resets every 28 days (05:00 UTC) Ranked Leagues Weekly Sign-up starts on Monday (05:00 UTC) Raid Weekend Weekly From Friday to Monday (07:00 UTC) Trader Weekly Offers reset every Tuesday (08:00 UTC)

A collection of frequently used Clash of Clans terms and abbreviations. It serves as a quick reference for both new and experienced players.
Term Definition Alt Alternate account of a player AQ Archer Queen BH Builder Hall BK Barbarian King CC Clan Castle CG Clan Games Charge One or more heroes are sent deep into the base to destroy key defenses or cc Coco loon Single Balloon sent ahead to trigger seeking air mines before main air troops or Healers CW Clan War CWL Clan War League DE Dark Elixir Dip Higher Town Hall attacking a lower Town Hall in war DPS Damage per second Engineering Keeping some defenses low or missing to reduce war weight while upgrading offense EQ Earthquake F2P Free‑to‑play player who spends no real money Farm (defence) Cluster of identical defenses such as Tesla farm or X‑Bow farm Farm(ing) Playing mainly to gain resources instead of trophies FC Friendly Challenge Funnel(ing) Removing outside buildings so troops or heroes walk into the base instead of around it Gemmer Player who progresses quickly by spending real money (gems) Hybrid Army that combines two troops, often Hog and Miner Global chat Removed worldwide chat channel that previously matched random players and helped find clans HP Hit points; total health of a troop or building Island Defense surrounded by empty space so troops can’t reach it Kill squad Small group of troops and heroes used to remove enemy Queen, Clan Castle, or key defenses at the beginning of an attack Meta “Most Effective Tactics Available”; often used for the strongest attack strategies Mirror Attacking the enemy base with the same map position number as your own in war Nerf Balance change that makes a troop, spell, or defense weaker Push Attacking to gain trophies and climb leagues Rushing Upgrading the Town Hall before heroes, troops, buildings and walls are fully upgraded Scout Attack used to reveal traps, pathing, and CC troops of a base Skelly Donut Skeleton or bat spells with rage and invisibility to destroy multiple defenses and clan castle in the core of the base Spam Simple attack without a plan and much funneling, often with a single troop Swag Dropping unnecessary spells/troops at the end of a guaranteed 3‑star to show overkill Tag Unique ID starting with # used to identify a specific clan or player Teaser base Base that exposes the Town Hall or other high‑value buildings to “bait” attackers into a trap‑heavy side TH Town Hall Tile Basic distance unit used for ranges and building sizes Troll Tesla Hidden Tesla placed in an unexpected corner or outside tile to waste time or surprise attackers WB Wall Breaker Walk Queen and / or Grand Warden with Healers clearing mostly outside buildings and creating a funnel War weight ”Strength” value of a base used for war matchmaking, driven by defenses, heroes, and troops

Each destroyed district adds a reward based on its Capital Hall or District Hall level. The offensive reward can be calculated by adding up the rewards for completely destroyed districts, dividing this value by the number of attacks, and multiplying it by 6.
Capital Hall Level Reward 2 180 3 360 4 585 5 810 6 1115 7 1240 8 1260 9 1375 10 1450 District Hall Level Reward 1 135 2 225 3 350 4 405 5 460 Too difficult? Let War Report do the math for you.

/about/blog/raid_weekend_rewards/.title: How are Raid Weekend rewards calculated?

Join my Discord server for app support, giveaways, and to chat with other War Report users.
You prefer to stay in the game? Join the in-game chat group .

Welcome to the documentation for War Report.
Use these pages to learn what the app can do and how to get the most out of it. Start with the Feature Overview and then read the FAQ for common questions.
Whether you’re managing Clan Wars, climbing the leaderboard of the Legends League, or aiming for a promotion of your clan in the Clan War League. War Report equips you with the tools to always stay one step ahead of your opponents. The app is completely free to use and adheres to the Safe and Fair Play Policy of the game by only using data from the official Clash of Clans API .
data.need-help#Need help?: If you have any questions, please join my Discord server or in-game chat group , or send me an email to support@warreportmail.app.

data.can-i-use-the-app-without-signing-in#Can: Yes. You can search for clans and players, view live wars, leagues, and statistics without an account. An account unlocks cross-device sync of bookmarks and push notifications — see the Account page for the full list.
data.can-my-account-get-banned-for-using-this: No. The app only uses data provided by Supercell. For more information, visit https://developer.clashofclans.com .
data.how-can-i-support-you#How can I support: I do not have a Creator Code yet, but you can still help by translating the app into additional languages. A positive review on Google Play or the App Store also helps a lot, and it is always appreciated if you tell your clan about the app.
data.how-do-i-delete-my-account#How do I dele: Open the More menu in the app and use the delete option, or go to warreport.app/more . Deletion removes the data we store about you on the backend; see the Privacy Policy for details.
data.i-found-a-bug--where-do-i-report-it#I fo: The fastest way is to drop a message in my Discord server . Email also works: support@warreportmail.app.
data.is-this-app-from-supercell#Is this app f: This material is unofficial and is not endorsed by Supercell. For more information, see Supercell’s Fan Content Policy: https://supercell.com/fan-content-policy .
data.the-app-is-not-available-in-my-country-w: Some countries block access to certain online services. If you are unable to use the app, you can try Cloudflare’s 1.1.1.1 DNS resolver or VPN app , which often helps reach blocked services.
data.what-is-clashking-and-why-does-the-app-r: The official Clash of Clans API only exposes a clan’s current war and a recent war log preview — it does not provide the full attack history for past wars or past CWL seasons. ClashKing is a community project that records this data and exposes it through an API. War Report uses it for past wars, past CWL seasons, and player name search.
data.why-are-the-legend-league-stats-for-my-a: War Report tries to track every player in Legend League I to show their full season of trophy gains and losses. The history can still be incomplete, for example if you are doing a lot of unranked attacks. For players in Legend League II or III, only the attacks and defenses from the recent battle log are shown.
data.why-cant-i-view-a-completed-cwl-season-r: ClashKing imports Clan War League results on the 13th of each month because clan wars of some groups do not finish until the 12th. Until that import is complete, the finished season is not available in the app.
data.why-isnt-the-data-exactly-up-to-date#Why:
The app retrieves data from the Clash of Clans API, which is cached. This can cause a short delay before the latest changes appear in the app. The table below shows the caching duration for the different API responses.
Type Caching Time Players 1 minute Clans 2 minutes Clan Wars 2 minutes Clan War League 10 minutes

data:
Explore the sections below to see how War Report helps you follow players, clans, wars, and more.
Players Clans Clan Wars Clan War League Clan Capital Battle Legend League Popular Armies Account API Embedding
Creating an account in the app settings unlocks additional features. You can sign in with email and password, your Discord account, or your Apple ID.
data.cross-device-sync#Cross-Device Sync: Your bookmarked players, clans, and other settings are automatically synchronized across all devices where you’re signed in. This makes it easy to switch between your phone and tablet without losing track of your favorite clans and players.
data.deleting-your-account#Deleting your acco: You can delete your account at any time from the app’s More menu, or via warreport.app/more . Deleting your account removes your synced data from the backend; see the Privacy Policy for details on retention.
data.notifications#Notifications:
Accounts enable push notifications to keep you informed about important clan activities. For example, you will receive a notification if you still need to attack in war, helping you never miss your chance to contribute. Notification preferences can be customized in the app settings to match your needs.
Notification preferences can be customized in the app settings to match your needs.

data: There’s a public API at https://api.warreport.app that serves additional Legend League and army statistics. The full contract is published as an OpenAPI 3.1 spec .
data.whats-available#What\u0026rsquo;s availa:
The Legend League rankings are an end-of-season snapshot; the army, hero, and equipment stats are refreshed daily:
Legend League rankings: global player and clan rankings, plus per-country breakdowns. Captured once at the end of each Legend season. Popular armies: the most-used army compositions among the top 200 Legend League players, with average stars and destruction. Updated daily. Heroes and hero equipment: usage statistics for heroes and hero equipment among top Legend League players. Updated daily. No API key is required. The data is free to use for anything. If the data shows up somewhere public, please credit War Report by mentioning the app and linking to https://warreport.app .
If you build something with it, I’d love to hear about it. Drop a message in the Discord server .

data: Open the player page and click the battle log button in the top-right corner to access this information. The Battle page shows the player’s recent battle log, ranking, and league history. It covers normal battles, ranked battles, and the Legend League.
data.history#History: The History tab displays the player’s league progression over time, including past ranked league finishes and Legend League seasons.
data.log#Log:
The Log tab lists every recent attack and defense, with stars, destruction, opponent, looted resources, and the army composition used.
Each attack entry in the battle log shows the full army composition used. A filter button on the battle log lets you filter entries by army type (Air or Ground) or by specific troops, spells, siege machines, or hero equipment, making it easy to find all attacks where a particular troop or strategy was used.
data.ranking#Ranking:
When the player is not in the Legend League, the Ranking tab shows the current standing within the player’s group.
When the player is in the Legend League, this tab is replaced by a Legend League tab that shows trophy progression for the current season.

data:
Click the menu button in the top right corner of the clan page to access this information. This page tracks your clan’s Clan Capital performance, showing detailed statistics for each Raid Weekend.
For the math behind raid medal payouts, see How are Raid Weekend rewards calculated?.
data.attacks#Attacks: A breakdown of every clan you raided, with the districts destroyed in each, the medals earned, and how the attacks were distributed across your members.
data.defenses#Defenses: How attacking clans performed against your Clan Capital, including which districts they destroyed and the medals you earned in defense.
data.summary#Summary: Overall metrics for the weekend — total raid medals earned, attacks used, districts destroyed, and Capital Gold looted. The list of your members shows each player’s attacks and individual stats.

data: If a clan war belongs to the Clan War League, you will find a button at the bottom of the clan war page that will take you to the clan war league page. For additional information about a clan or player, click on an item in the group or player list.
data.past-seasons#Past seasons: The app also provides details of past Clan War League seasons. Simply click on an entry in the war log to view them. The data is provided by the ClashKing Discord bot. Please note that some Clan War League months may not have been tracked by ClashKing.
data.whats-on-the-page#What\u0026rsquo;s on t: Group standings for the clans in the group with stars and destruction. Round results for each of the seven war days. Player rankings for the entire group, sortable by stars, destruction, or attacks used. Clan and player stats when you tap on an individual clan or player. Earned medals (both base medals and bonus medals) per clan and per player. See How are Clan War League medals calculated? for the math behind these numbers.

data:
The Wars tab displays ongoing clan wars of your bookmarked players and clans. Use the filter in the top right corner if you only want to see specific war types. You can also exclude individual clans or players from the list. If you have many bookmarks, loading the list may take longer, so use the filter to select the wars you are interested in. As soon as a player or clan is at war, you will also find a button at the bottom of the player or clan page that will take you to the clan war page.
For friendly wars and regular clan wars, your clan must have a public war log. If your war log is private, ask your leader to change the clan settings. For the Clan War League, a public war log is not required.
data.past-wars#Past wars: The app also provides details of past clan wars. Simply click on an entry in the war log to view them. The data is provided by the ClashKing Discord bot. Please note that some clan wars may not have been tracked by ClashKing.

data: Clan details, such as a list of all members and the number of wins, losses, and ties in clan wars, are displayed on the clan page. For additional information, click one of the buttons in the top-right corner to access pages with detailed statistics on recent Clan Wars, the Clan War League, and the Clan Capital.
data.bookmark-clans#Bookmark clans: Bookmark your favorite clans by clicking the bookmark button in the top-right corner of their detail page. To remove a bookmark, simply click the button again or swipe the clan entry in your bookmarks list to the left or right. You can also rearrange your bookmarks to keep them organized: tap and hold any item, then drag it to the desired position.
data.raid-weekend#Raid Weekend: This page tracks your clan’s Clan Capital performance, showing detailed statistics for each raid weekend.
data.search-clans#Search clans:
To search a clan, click on the button in the bottom right corner of the clans page. You can search clans by tag or by name.
The image below shows where you can find the clan tag. Click on your experience (1) to open your profile, then click on your clan (2), and then copy the tag (3) below your name.
data.stats#Stats: The Stats page presents aggregated war and CWL performance statistics over a selected time period for the clan and all of its members.
data.war-log#War log: This page displays the clan’s Clan War and CWL results.

data: War Report can display any single page on its own, with the app navigation stripped away, so you can embed it on your own website. This is useful for clan sites, blogs, or fan pages that want to show live data without sending visitors elsewhere.
data.example-embed-a-player-page#Example: emb:
Every player has a page at https://warreport.app/players/, where is the player tag without the leading #. For example, the player with the tag #8UYVP00PV lives at https://warreport.app/players/8UYVP00PV .
To embed that player page, add ?embed=1 and drop the URL into an iframe:
<iframe src="https://warreport.app/players/8UYVP00PV?embed=1" width="400" height="700" style="border: none;" title="War Report player profile"> </iframe> Adjust the width and height to fit your layout. The same approach also works for other pages such as clans, wars, and rankings. Just append ?embed=1 to the URL.
data.how-it-works#How it works:
Take the normal web address of the page you want to show and add ?embed=1 to the end, then load that URL inside an .
In embed mode the bottom navigation, navigation rail, and back button are hidden, so the embedded view stays put and visitors always see exactly the page you embedded. Tapping a link that would normally open another page (a clan, a player, a war, and so on) instead opens that page in the full War Report app in a new browser tab, so the embed itself never navigates away. A small link back to War Report is shown in the corner.

data:
Click the button in the top right corner of the player page to access this information. This page displays the player’s Legend League performance for the current season. It shows trophies gained and lost per day along with a graph visualizing trophy progression throughout the month.
The information may not include every attack and defense. War Report tries to track every player in Legend League I to show their full season of trophy gains and losses. The history can still be incomplete, for example if you are doing a lot of unranked attacks. For players in Legend League II or III, only the attacks and defenses from the recent battle log are shown.

Player details, such as hero and troop levels, are displayed on the player page. For additional information, click one of the buttons in the top-right corner to access pages with detailed statistics on recent Clan Wars and the Legend League.
data.achievements#Achievements: The Achievements page displays the player’s progress across all in-game achievements, organized by category (Home Village, Builder Base, Clan Capital).
data.armies#Armies:
The Armies tab shows every army composition the player has used in their recent battle log, grouped by dominant troop. This includes Home Village, ranked, and Legend League battles. Each card displays the troop composition, average stars, average destruction, and total number of attacks.
When a player has used multiple variations of the same dominant-troop army (e.g. several different Dragon compositions), a variants button appears. Tapping it opens a sheet listing each variant with its own stats.
Every army card has an Open in-game button, letting you copy that exact composition into the game with a single tap.
data.attacks#Attacks: This page lists every clan war and CWL attack and defense the player has made. Use the filter at the top to include or exclude friendly wars.
data.battle#Battle: This page displays the player’s Battle log, ranking, and league history, covering normal attacks, ranked attacks, and Legend League attacks.
data.bookmark-players#Bookmark players: Bookmark your favorite players by clicking the bookmark button in the top-right corner of their detail page. To remove a bookmark, simply click the button again or swipe the player entry in your bookmarks list to the left or right. You can also rearrange your bookmarks to keep them organized: tap and hold any item, then drag it to the desired position.
data.previous-clans#Previous Clans: This page shows previous clans the player has been a member of and the total time spent in each clan.
data.search-players#Search players:
To search for a player, click on the button in the bottom right corner of the players page. You can search players by tag or by name. The search by name is based on data provided by the ClashKing Discord bot and may not include all players.
The image below shows where you can find the player tag. Click on your experience (1) to open your profile (2), and then copy the tag (3) below your name.
data.stats#Stats: The Stats page presents aggregated war and CWL performance statistics over a selected time period.

data: Open the Popular Armies page from the More menu. It lists the most used army compositions of the top 200 Legend League players. Each card shows the dominant troop name, total usage count, average stars, and average destruction. Tapping an army opens a detail page listing Legend League players who used it, sorted by average stars and destruction.
data.army-link#Army link: Each army card has an Open in-game button that copies the exact composition into the game with a single tap.

data: This policy explains how the War Report app and warreport.app process personal data. It may be updated as the service evolves. Last updated: 2026-05-07.
data.authentication#Authentication: Email + hashed password, or your Discord (Discord Netherlands B.V., NL) or Apple (Apple Distribution International Ltd, IE) user ID. With third-party login you authenticate on the provider’s site; we receive your user ID and, if you grant it, your email. Retained while your account exists; deleted with the account. Legal basis: Art. 6(1)(b) GDPR.
data.cdn--dns-cloudflare#CDN / DNS (Cloudflar: Cloudflare, Inc.; IP and request metadata, processed for delivery and attack mitigation. Transfers outside the EU are covered by Standard Contractual Clauses. Legal basis: Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR (legitimate interest in secure, performant content delivery and protection against attacks).
data.clashking-api#ClashKing API: The app calls api.clashk.ing directly to fetch in-game data; your IP and request metadata reach the ClashKing operator. Legal basis: Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR (legitimate interest in providing the in-game data you requested).
data.contact-requests#Contact requests: The data you send us by email, used only to answer you. Emails are stored in Apple iCloud Mail (Apple Distribution International Ltd, IE); we do not store contact emails on our backend. US transfers are covered by Apple’s EU-US Data Privacy Framework certification and Standard Contractual Clauses. Retained as long as needed. Legal basis: Art. 6(1)(b) GDPR.
data.controller#Controller: Colin Schmale, Sülldorfer Mühlenweg 34, 22589 Hamburg, Germany. support@warreportmail.app
data.error-monitoring-sentry#Error monitoring: Functional Software, Inc. (Sentry); anonymized device and browser data; IP and location are stripped. EU data residency, retention ≤ 90 days. Standard Contractual Clauses cover transfers to the US. Legal basis: Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR (legitimate interest in identifying and fixing software defects).
data.hosting-hetzner#Hosting (Hetzner): Hetzner Online GmbH; the backend storing your account data, server logs, and push tokens runs on a virtual server in Ashburn, VA, USA. Standard Contractual Clauses cover the US transfer. Legal basis: Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR (legitimate interest in operating the service on reliable infrastructure).
data.push-notifications#Push notifications: Firebase Cloud Messaging token, stored on our backend to deliver the alerts you subscribe to. Retained until you disable notifications or delete your account. Processor: Google Ireland Ltd; transfers to the US are covered by Standard Contractual Clauses. Legal basis: Art. 6(1)(a) GDPR (consent via the system notification permission), revocable at any time in your device settings.
data.server-logs#Server logs: URL, time, OS, IP. Used to run, secure, and debug the service. Retained 30 days. Legal basis: Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR (legitimate interest in stable, secure operation).
data.what-we-process#What we process: No data is sold and no automated decision-making takes place. Providing your data is voluntary, but account features cannot be used without it. The service is not directed to children under 16; by creating an account you confirm you are at least 16 years old.
data.your-rights#Your rights:
Under Art. 15–21 GDPR you have rights of access, rectification, erasure, restriction, portability, and objection. You may withdraw any consent at any time (Art. 7(3) GDPR). To exercise a right, email the address above. You can delete your account at any time at warreport.app/more .
Right to object (Art. 21 GDPR): You may at any time, on grounds relating to your particular situation, object to processing based on Art. 6(1)(f) (server logs, Sentry, Cloudflare, ClashKing).
You may also lodge a complaint with Der Hamburgische Beauftragte für Datenschutz und Informationsfreiheit (HmbBfDI), Ludwig-Erhard-Str. 22, 20459 Hamburg — datenschutz-hamburg.de .
